adjacent_excluded_source.reason: Separate focused attack suite with one SIM_ID, one result family, different schema, and different catalog placement.

notes: This batch treats run_batch_v3.py as a composite precursor bundle rather than as one coherent present-tense executable family.
The adjacent engine32 source was read and explicitly excluded from this batch.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Shows batch_v3 under OTHER while later descendant families and engine32 are placed elsewhere in the catalog.
Records later standalone descendants but not the bundle-local V3/V1 SIM_IDs; also preserves Stage16 and negctrl version drift.
Axis12 descendant becomes a parameter-grid surface rather than the bundle's sequence-count snapshot.
Axis0 descendant collapses into a single standalone surface.
Axis0 descendant reshapes into a two-sequence delta surface.
Stage16 descendant extracted as standalone uniform surface.
Stage16 descendant duplicate with same stored bytes under a different SIM_ID/name.
Negctrl descendant with 512 trials and separate provenance residue.
Paired result for the adjacent excluded next-family source.

runtime_expectations: run_batch_v3.py writes one aggregate results_batch_v3.json plus one sim_evidence_pack.txt with 4 SIM_EVIDENCE blocks.
The four embedded SIM_IDs are S_SIM_AXIS12_CHANNEL_REALIZATION_V3, S_SIM_STAGE16_SUB4_AXIS6_UNIFORM_V3, S_SIM_AXIS0_TRAJ_CORR_SUITE_V3, and S_SIM_NEGCTRL_AXIS6_COMMUTE_V1.
Evidence hashes are computed per embedded payload rather than from the aggregate results_batch_v3.json container.
The paired aggregate result file stores four top-level objects keyed by SIM_ID.
failure_modes: The aggregate results_batch_v3.json file can be mistaken for the evidence-hashed output even though the evidence model is payload-level.
Later standalone descendants can be mistaken for exact renames of the bundle payloads even though their schemas drift by family.
Stage16 duplicate descendants can be collapsed incorrectly because V4 and V5 have identical stored bytes but different labels.
Negctrl descendants can be treated as identical because their mean metrics match even though trials and provenance differ.
Adjacent engine32 can be merged incorrectly into this bundle on the basis of shared axis vocabulary.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
